title: Vietnamese Pho
description: A traditional Vietnamese noodle soup made with a rich beef broth and served with tender beef and rice noodles. Pho is a comforting and flavorful dish that is perfect for a cold day.

ingredients.name: Beef bones
ingredients.notes: You can use beef neck bones or oxtail for a richer broth

ingredients.name: Rice noodles
ingredients.notes: You can find rice noodles at most Asian markets

ingredients.name: Pho spice mix
ingredients.notes: You can make your own pho spice mix with cinnamon, star anise, and cloves

ingredients.name: Vietnamese oyster sauce
ingredients.notes: You can omit this if you don't have it or prefer not to use it

ingredients.name: Beef brisket or chuck
ingredients.notes: You can use either beef brisket or chuck for this recipe

ingredients.name: Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: You can use any type of mushroom you like

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: You can use either a yellow onion or a white onion

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: You can use either fresh or frozen ginger

instructions: 1. In a large pot, char the onion and ginger over medium heat until they are caramelized.
2. Add the beef bones to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
3. Add 4 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
4. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
5. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.
6. Add the pho spice mix, oyster sauce, and mushrooms to the pot.
8. Slice the beef against the grain and add it to the pot.
10. Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
11. Assemble the pho by placing the noodles in a bowl, then adding the beef and broth.
12. Garnish with fresh herbs, such as basil or mint, and serve immediately.
